name: Phase 8: Adaptive Memory Lifecycle
summary: Three-dimensional memory taxonomy with lifecycle phases and intelligent forgetting. Extends Supermemory with factual, experiential, and working memory types, decay functions, relevance-based retention, and memory health monitoring.
researchBasis: Three-dimensional memory taxonomy (factual, experiential, working) with lifecycle phases (Formation, Evolution, Retrieval). Forgetting as feature engineering with decay functions and relevance-based retention.

userStories.title: Define memory type enums
userStories.description: As a user, I want memories categorized by type (factual, experiential, working) so that the system can apply appropriate retention strategies to different kinds of knowledge.
acceptanceCriteria: MemoryType enum defined with FACTUAL, EXPERIENTIAL, WORKING values
Each memory record in Supermemory has a type field
Migration adds type column to existing memories table with default value
Type detection heuristics classify new memories automatically

userStories.title: Add memory decay functions with configurable half-lives
userStories.description: As a user, I want memories to decay over time with configurable half-lives so that outdated information naturally fades while important memories persist.
acceptanceCriteria: Decay function implemented with exponential decay curve
Configurable half-life per memory type (factual: 90 days, experiential: 30 days, working: 7 days)
Decay score stored and updated on each memory access
Half-life configuration exposed via settings

userStories.title: Implement relevance scoring for memory retention
userStories.description: As a user, I want memories scored by relevance so that frequently accessed and contextually important memories are retained while less relevant ones decay faster.
acceptanceCriteria: Relevance score calculated from access frequency, recency, and context match
Score range 0.0-1.0 with configurable thresholds
Relevance boost applied when memory is accessed or referenced
Low-relevance memories flagged for potential pruning

userStories.title: Create memory consolidation pipeline
userStories.description: As a user, I want a consolidation pipeline that processes memories through Formation, Evolution, and Retrieval phases so that memories mature and strengthen over time.
acceptanceCriteria: MemoryPhase enum with FORMATION, EVOLUTION, RETRIEVAL states
Formation phase: new memories created with initial decay and relevance
Evolution phase: memories strengthened by repeated access, consolidated with related memories
Retrieval phase: mature memories optimized for fast recall

userStories.title: Add forgetting mechanism with time-based pruning
userStories.description: As a user, I want an intelligent forgetting mechanism that prunes low-value memories so that the memory system stays lean and relevant without manual cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: Scheduled pruning job runs daily/weekly
Prune threshold configurable (default: decay < 0.1 AND relevance < 0.2)
Pruned memories archived before deletion (soft delete with 30-day recovery)
Pruning summary logged with count and categories removed

userStories.title: Implement memory replay for high-value items
userStories.description: As a user, I want high-value memories to be replayed periodically so that important knowledge is reinforced and doesn't decay unnecessarily.
acceptanceCriteria: Replay mechanism triggers for memories with high relevance but declining decay
Spaced repetition algorithm determines replay intervals
Replay events boost decay score back toward 1.0
Integration with existing sm review command

userStories.title: Create memory lifecycle dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dashboard showing memory lifecycle statistics so that I can understand how my knowledge base is evolving and make informed decisions about retention policies.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows memory count by type and phase
Visualization of decay distribution across memories
List of memories approaching prune threshold
CLI command: sm lifecycle or sm-dash

userStories.title: Add memory health metrics and alerts
userStories.description: As a user, I want health metrics and alerts for my memory system so that I'm notified of issues like excessive pruning, memory bloat, or consolidation failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Health metrics: total memories, average decay, prune rate, consolidation success rate
Alert thresholds configurable in settings
Alerts for: >10% pruned in single run, <50 total memories, consolidation failures
Health check command: sm health with pass/warn/fail status
